task.title: Editing a sequential data set or partitioned data set member

titlealts.searchtitle: Editing a sequential data set or PDS member

task.shortdesc: You can edit a sequential data set or partitioned data set member from either the Remote Systems view or the view.

context.note:
Opening large files (greater than 2500 kilobytes) can cause out-of-memory errors and a forced shutdown of the workbench. Use the Show warning message on opening files setting of the z/OS Solutions preference page to check the size of any file that is being opened and display a warning if it is larger than the specified threshold.
You can check the size of a file before you open it by opening the Properties view. This view shows the file size for remote resources in bytes (for resources that are less than 1000 bytes) or in kilobytes (for resources that are greater than or equal to 1000 bytes).

step.cmd: To edit a sequential data set or partitioned data set member, you must first locate the member in either the Remote Systems view or the view:

choices.choice: In the Remote Systems view, connect to the z/OS system and expand the MVS Files folder (or any other filter that contains the file). Existing data sets associated with the high-level qualifier are listed in this folder.

In the view, expand the project and subproject in which the file is located. If you do not find the data set you want to edit, you can add it to a subproject from the Remote Systems view. For instructions, see the related tasks.

step.cmd: After you locate the sequential data set or partitioned data set member in either view, either double-click it or right-click it and select Open to begin editing it in the default editor.
step.stepresult: An outline of the data set contents is also displayed in the Outline view.
